question: [File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `hiero.NewTransferTransaction()` does not set a `SetTransactionMemo()` with a unique identifier, making it impossible to distinguish legitimate pinger transactions from attacker-injected transactions in the mirror node's transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `min(d, 30 * time.Second)` cap in `backoff()` to predict the exact retry timing of `submitWithRetry()`, and use this knowledge to time a network partition attack that ensures all retry attempts fail, causing the pinger to exhaust retries and miss a health-check window?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `hiero.AccountIDFromString(cfg.toAccountID)` is called on every invocation of `submitWithRetry()` (every tick) rather than once at startup, and by rapidly changing `cfg.toAccountID` via a race condition in a shared config store, cause different ticks to send to different destination accounts?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a `SetNodeAccountIDs()` call on `hiero.NewTransferTransaction()`, allowing the SDK to select any node from the network map, and by poisoning the network map via a tampered `HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST` response, ensure all transactions are routed to a single malicious node?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user who can read the pinger's stdout/stderr logs extract the operator account ID from `log.Printf(\

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `cryptoTransfer.Execute(client)` returns a `TransactionResponse` without verifying the transaction was actually submitted to the intended node, allowing a compromised SDK or network layer to return a fake `resp` with a valid-looking `TransactionID` while the actual transaction was never submitted?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user set `HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_AMOUNT_TINYBAR` to `1` (minimum), causing each transaction to still incur the full network fee, making the fee-to-transfer ratio extremely high and effectively turning the pinger into a pure fee-drain mechanism on the operator account?
